title: Whole Unit Protection
shortDescription: A general system protection has been activated.
fullDescription: Error E0 on Gree air conditioners is a general protection code indicating the system has detected a condition requiring shutdown. It is often a precursor to more specific error codes.
symptoms: May transition to a more specific error

fixSteps.title: Reset the system
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at breaker for 5 minutes. Restart and note if a different, more specific error appears.

fixSteps.title: Clean filters and condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the indoor air filter and outdoor condenser coil to rule out airflow issues.

fixSteps.title: Check power supply
fixSteps.instruction: Verify supply voltage is within the rated range for your model.

fixSteps.title: Monitor for recurring errors
fixSteps.instruction: If E0 keeps appearing, note the circumstances and contact a Gree service technician.

whenToCallPro: If E0 recurs frequently after basic troubleshooting.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC E0 whole unit protection error fix

faq.q: Is E0 a specific error?
faq.a: E0 is a general protection code. A more specific error code often follows after a reset.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause E0?
faq.a: Yes, power fluctuations commonly trigger E0.

faq.q: Should I be worried about E0?
faq.a: A single occurrence after a power event is normal. Recurring E0 needs investigation.

title: High Pressure Protection
shortDescription: The high-pressure switch has tripped due to excessive system pressure.
fullDescription: Error E1 indicates the refrigerant circuit high-pressure cutout has activated. The compressor stops to prevent damage.

fixSteps.title: Clean outdoor condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Wash the condenser coil with a garden hose. Remove debris and ensure 18 inches clearance.

fixSteps.title: Check outdoor fan
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the outdoor fan spins when the unit is running. If not, check the capacitor.

fixSteps.title: Reset after cleaning
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off for 30 minutes to reset the high-pressure switch, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Professional pressure check
fixSteps.instruction: If E1 recurs, a technician should measure system pressures with gauges.

whenToCallPro: If the error recurs after condenser cleaning.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC E1 high pressure error condenser cleaning fix

faq.q: What is high pressure in an AC?
faq.a: Head pressure above the design limit, usually caused by poor heat rejection at the condenser.

faq.q: Can I reset E1 myself?
faq.a: Yes, clean the condenser, turn off for 30 minutes, then restart.

faq.q: How often should I clean the condenser?
faq.a: At least twice a year, more often in dusty environments.

title: Indoor Unit Anti-Freeze Protection
shortDescription: The indoor coil temperature has dropped below the freeze protection threshold.
fullDescription: Error E2 activates when the evaporator coil temperature falls too low, indicating potential icing. The system stops to prevent damage.

causes.cause: Indoor fan motor running too slowly

fixSteps.title: Clean the air filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and wash the indoor air filter. Let it dry completely before reinstalling.

fixSteps.title: Set fan to auto
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure fan speed is on auto, not low. Low fan speed reduces airflow over the coil.

fixSteps.title: Defrost the coil
fixSteps.instruction: Run fan-only mode for 30 minutes to melt any ice.

fixSteps.title: Check refrigerant if recurring
fixSteps.instruction: If E2 returns after filter cleaning, refrigerant may be low due to a leak.

whenToCallPro: If E2 persists after cleaning the filter.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC E2 freeze protection ice coil fix

faq.q: Why is my indoor unit freezing?
faq.a: The most common cause is a dirty air filter restricting airflow. Clean it immediately.

faq.q: Can I scrape ice off the coil?
faq.a: No, use fan-only mode to melt the ice naturally. Scraping can damage the coil fins.

faq.q: How often should I clean the filter?
faq.a: Every 2-4 weeks during the cooling season.

title: Low Pressure Protection
shortDescription: The low-pressure switch has been triggered due to abnormally low system pressure.
fullDescription: Error E3 indicates the low-pressure cutout has activated, typically caused by a refrigerant leak or restriction.

causes.cause: Blocked indoor coil due to dirt

fixSteps.title: Clean indoor coil and filter
fixSteps.instruction: A severely dirty indoor coil can mimic low pressure conditions. Clean thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Check for ice
fixSteps.instruction: Look for ice on the indoor coil or suction line. Ice restricts flow and drops pressure.

fixSteps.title: Look for oil stains
fixSteps.instruction: Check refrigerant connections for oil stains indicating a refrigerant leak.

fixSteps.title: Professional leak test
fixSteps.instruction: A certified technician should pressure test the system, locate and fix leaks, then recharge.

whenToCallPro: If the system has a refrigerant leak.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC E3 low pressure refrigerant leak fix

faq.q: Does E3 mean I need refrigerant?
faq.a: Usually yes, E3 indicates low refrigerant. But a dirty coil should be ruled out first.

faq.q: How much does a leak repair cost?
faq.a: Depending on leak location, expect $200-$600 including refrigerant recharge.

faq.q: How long does refrigerant last?
faq.a: In a properly sealed system, refrigerant lasts the life of the unit. It only decreases if there is a leak.

title: Compressor Discharge Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The compressor discharge temperature has exceeded the safety limit.
fullDescription: Error E4 indicates the compressor outlet temperature has risen above the maximum allowed value, triggering protection shutdown.

fixSteps.title: Clean the condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Thoroughly wash the outdoor condenser coil and clear obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Check airflow
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure outdoor unit has adequate ventilation clearance on all sides.

fixSteps.title: Allow cooling period
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off for 30 minutes to let the compressor cool before restarting.

fixSteps.title: Professional check
fixSteps.instruction: If E4 recurs, refrigerant levels and the expansion valve need professional inspection.

whenToCallPro: If E4 persists after cleaning.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC E4 compressor discharge high temperature fix

faq.q: What is discharge temperature?
faq.a: The temperature of hot gas leaving the compressor. Normal is 60-90C, above 120C is dangerous.

faq.q: Can running the AC at maximum on the hottest days cause E4?
faq.a: Yes, extreme ambient temperatures can push the system to its limits.

faq.q: Is E4 damaging to the compressor?
faq.a: The E4 protection prevents damage by shutting down before harm occurs. Address the root cause promptly.

title: Overcurrent Protection
shortDescription: The compressor or system is drawing excessive electrical current.
fullDescription: Error E5 means the current sensor has detected amperage above the safe limit. The system shuts down to protect wiring and the compressor.

causes.cause: Dirty condenser causing high head pressure

symptoms: Compressor attempts to start then stops

fixSteps.title: Check voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure supply voltage at the outdoor unit. Must be within rated range.

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Wash the condenser coil to reduce head pressure and compressor load.

fixSteps.title: Test capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Check the compressor run capacitor. Replace if weak.

fixSteps.title: Measure compressor amps
fixSteps.instruction: Use a clamp meter to check running current against nameplate rating.

whenToCallPro: If the compressor itself is drawing excessive current.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC E5 overcurrent error fix capacitor

faq.q: Can low voltage cause E5?
faq.a: Yes, low voltage causes the compressor to draw more current to maintain output.

faq.q: Should I keep resetting after E5?
faq.a: Diagnose the cause first. Repeated starts with overcurrent can damage the compressor.

faq.q: What causes high compressor current?
faq.a: High head pressure, low voltage, failing capacitor, or compressor motor degradation.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication between the indoor and outdoor units has failed.
fullDescription: Error E6 on Gree units indicates a loss of serial communication between the indoor and outdoor control boards.
symptoms: Indoor fan runs but no cooling

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at breaker for 5 minutes. Restart both units.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect communication wires at both indoor and outdoor terminals. Tighten connections.

fixSteps.title: Test continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Test each wire between units for continuity with a multimeter.

fixSteps.title: PCB diagnosis
fixSteps.instruction: If wiring is good, one of the PCBs needs professional diagnosis.

whenToCallPro: If wiring is intact but communication fails.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC E6 communication error fix wiring

faq.q: Is E6 common on Gree?
faq.a: Yes, E6 is one of the most commonly reported Gree errors, usually from loose wiring.

faq.q: Can installation issues cause E6?
faq.a: Yes, improper wiring during installation is a frequent cause.

faq.q: How many wires connect the units?
faq.a: Typically 3-4 wires for power and communication.

title: Indoor Fan Motor Overcurrent or Fault
shortDescription: The indoor fan motor is drawing excessive current or has stalled.
fullDescription: Error E8 indicates the indoor fan motor is not operating within normal parameters. The motor may be locked, stalled, or have a winding fault.
symptoms: Indoor fan not spinning or spinning slowly

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the indoor unit and look for objects blocking the fan wheel.

fixSteps.title: Spin the fan manually
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, try to rotate the fan. It should move freely without grinding.

fixSteps.title: Clean the fan wheel
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the blower wheel (barrel fan). Dust buildup increases motor load.

fixSteps.title: Test or replace motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the fan is clean and unobstructed but does not run, the motor or its capacitor needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the motor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC E8 indoor fan motor error fix

faq.q: Can dust cause E8?
faq.a: Yes, heavy dust on the blower wheel increases motor load and can trigger E8.

faq.q: How do I clean the blower wheel?
faq.a: Open the indoor unit, remove the wheel, and clean with a brush and mild detergent.

faq.q: How often should the blower wheel be cleaned?
faq.a: At least once a year, more in dusty environments.

title: Refrigerant Shortage Detection
shortDescription: The system has detected insufficient refrigerant in the circuit.
fullDescription: Error F0 indicates the Gree system's built-in refrigerant detection has determined the charge is too low for safe and efficient operation.

fixSteps.title: Visual leak check
fixSteps.instruction: Look for oil stains at all refrigerant line connections, service valves, and coils.

fixSteps.title: Clean filters
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the indoor filter to rule out airflow issues that can mimic refrigerant shortage.

fixSteps.title: Reset and test
fixSteps.instruction: Reset the system. If F0 returns within an hour of running, a genuine refrigerant issue exists.

fixSteps.title: Professional service
fixSteps.instruction: A technician must find the leak, repair it, evacuate, and recharge the system.

whenToCallPro: If F0 persists after cleaning filters.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC F0 refrigerant shortage leak fix

faq.q: Can I add refrigerant myself?
faq.a: No, refrigerant handling requires EPA certification and professional equipment.

faq.q: How do I know if I have a leak?
faq.a: Oil stains at connections, gradually declining cooling, and ice on the indoor coil are signs of a leak.

faq.q: Is a leak repair covered by warranty?
faq.a: Manufacturing defects in coils are often covered. Installation flare connection leaks may not be.

title: Indoor Coil Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The indoor evaporator coil thermistor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Error F1 indicates the evaporator coil temperature sensor is reading abnormally due to an open circuit, short circuit, or drift.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off for 5 minutes and restart.

fixSteps.title: Check connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open indoor unit and verify the coil sensor connector is secure.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: At 25C expect approximately 10 kOhms. Replace if abnormal.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install a matching thermistor and clip to the evaporator coil.

whenToCallPro: If the PCB is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC F1 coil sensor error fix

faq.q: Is the coil sensor expensive?
faq.a: No, thermistors are very affordable at $5-$15.

faq.q: Can I use a generic sensor?
faq.a: Use a sensor with matching resistance characteristics for your Gree model.

faq.q: How do I know which sensor is faulty?
faq.a: F1 specifically refers to the indoor coil sensor.

title: Outdoor Coil Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The outdoor condenser coil thermistor is malfunctioning.
fullDescription: Error F2 indicates the outdoor condenser coil temperature sensor has failed, affecting defrost control and condenser protection.
symptoms: Outdoor unit icing in heating mode
System shuts down in extreme temperatures

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Inspect sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Find the sensor on the outdoor condenser coil. Check for damage and attachment.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Measure at ambient temperature. Replace if out of range.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Clip new sensor to coil and route wire safely.

whenToCallPro: If outdoor unit access is unsafe.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC F2 outdoor coil sensor fix

faq.q: How long do outdoor sensors last?
faq.a: 5-10 years typically, shorter in coastal or harsh environments.

faq.q: Does F2 affect cooling?
faq.a: Yes, without condenser monitoring the system cannot protect itself properly.

faq.q: Can I access the outdoor sensor myself?
faq.a: If the unit is at ground level, yes. For elevated installations, use a technician.

title: Discharge Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The compressor discharge temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: Error F3 means the sensor monitoring the compressor hot gas discharge temperature is reading abnormally.

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the condenser is clean and airflow is good to rule out genuine overheating.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor attachment
fixSteps.instruction: Find the sensor on the compressor discharge pipe and verify it is securely attached.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Compare sensor reading to Gree specifications. Replace if out of range.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install new sensor with thermal paste for good contact on the discharge pipe.

whenToCallPro: If the compressor is genuinely overheating.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC F3 discharge sensor error fix

faq.q: What is discharge temperature?
faq.a: The temperature of the hot gas leaving the compressor, typically 60-90C normal.

faq.q: Can a faulty F3 sensor cause shutdown?
faq.a: Yes, if the sensor reads too high, the system will shut down to protect the compressor.

faq.q: Is F3 urgent?
faq.a: Address promptly to prevent unnecessary compressor cycling.

title: Outdoor Ambient Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The outdoor ambient air temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: Error F4 indicates the outdoor ambient temperature thermistor is reading abnormally, affecting the system's ability to adapt to outdoor conditions.
symptoms: System performance affected in extreme weather
Unit may refuse to start in very hot or cold conditions

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor placement
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure the sensor is shaded from direct sun and away from heat sources.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Approximately 10 kOhms at 25C. Replace if out of range.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install matching replacement, ensuring proper shaded location.

whenToCallPro: If the outdoor PCB is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC F4 ambient sensor error fix

faq.q: Does the ambient sensor affect AC performance?
faq.a: Yes, it helps the system optimize for current outdoor conditions.

faq.q: Can sun exposure damage the sensor?
faq.a: Direct sun can cause misleading readings but usually does not damage the sensor itself.

faq.q: Where is the ambient sensor?
faq.a: Typically mounted on or near the outdoor unit housing.

title: Indoor Room Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The indoor unit room temperature sensor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: Error F5 indicates the room air temperature thermistor is open, shorted, or reading out of range.

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Power off 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Open indoor unit, find the room sensor near air intake, and check connector.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: About 10 kOhms at 25C. Replace if abnormal.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Install a matching thermistor.

whenToCallPro: If the PCB is suspected.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC F5 room temperature sensor fix

faq.q: Can a faulty room sensor cause high bills?
faq.a: Yes, if the sensor reads wrong, the AC may run nonstop.

faq.q: Where is the room sensor?
faq.a: Near the air return intake on the indoor unit.

faq.q: How much does the sensor cost?
faq.a: Very affordable at $5-$15.

title: Defrost Mode Active
shortDescription: The outdoor unit has entered defrost mode to melt ice from the condenser coil.
fullDescription: H1 is not actually an error but a status indicator. During heating mode in cold weather, the outdoor coil can ice up. H1 indicates the system is running a defrost cycle.

causes.cause: Normal defrost cycle in heating mode
Low outdoor temperature causing frequent icing
Faulty defrost sensor triggering unnecessary cycles

symptoms: Steam may rise from outdoor unit

fixSteps.title: Wait for defrost to complete
fixSteps.instruction: H1 is normal. Wait 5-10 minutes for the defrost cycle to complete. The system will resume heating automatically.

fixSteps.title: Check outdoor coil
fixSteps.instruction: After defrost completes, check if the outdoor coil is clear of ice.

fixSteps.title: Ensure good airflow
fixSteps.instruction: Clear any snow, leaves, or debris from around the outdoor unit that could contribute to icing.

fixSteps.title: Monitor frequency
fixSteps.instruction: If defrost cycles happen every 10-15 minutes, have a technician check refrigerant and sensor.

whenToCallPro: Only if defrost cycles are excessively frequent.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC H1 defrost mode normal operation

faq.q: Is H1 an error?
faq.a: No, H1 is a normal status showing the defrost cycle is active.

faq.q: How long does defrost take?
faq.a: Typically 5-10 minutes.

faq.q: Should I worry about H1?
faq.a: No, unless it happens every 10-15 minutes, which could indicate a problem.

title: Compressor Overload Protection
shortDescription: The compressor internal or external overload protector has tripped.
fullDescription: Error H3 indicates the compressor overload device has activated due to excessive temperature or current.

fixSteps.title: Clean condenser
fixSteps.instruction: Wash the outdoor condenser coil thoroughly.

fixSteps.title: Check voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Verify supply voltage at outdoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Wait and reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off for 30 minutes for the overload to reset, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Check capacitor
fixSteps.instruction: Test compressor capacitor and replace if weak.

whenToCallPro: If the error persists after cleaning and capacitor check.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC H3 compressor overload fix

faq.q: Is H3 the same as E5?
faq.a: Similar but different. H3 is the thermal/mechanical overload, E5 is the electronic overcurrent sensor.

faq.q: How long to wait for overload reset?
faq.a: At least 30 minutes for the thermal overload to cool down.

faq.q: Can I bypass the overload protector?
faq.a: Absolutely not. It is a critical safety device protecting the compressor.

title: IPM Module Protection
shortDescription: The Intelligent Power Module in the inverter has triggered its protection circuit.
fullDescription: Error H5 indicates the IPM (Intelligent Power Module) that drives the compressor has detected an internal fault or overcurrent condition.

fixSteps.title: Reset the system
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at breaker for 10 minutes for full inverter discharge and reset.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inverter heatsink
fixSteps.instruction: Remove outdoor unit cover and clean dust from the IPM heatsink fins with compressed air or a soft brush.

fixSteps.title: Check IPM connections
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the connections between the IPM module and the compressor wiring.

fixSteps.title: Professional IPM replacement
fixSteps.instruction: If the IPM module has failed, it must be replaced by a qualified technician.

whenToCallPro: If the IPM module needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC H5 IPM module protection error fix

faq.q: What is the IPM module?
faq.a: The Intelligent Power Module converts DC power to the variable AC that drives the inverter compressor.

faq.q: Can I replace the IPM myself?
faq.a: Not recommended. It handles high voltage and requires careful handling and testing.

faq.q: Is H5 expensive to repair?
faq.a: IPM replacement typically costs $200-$500 including labor.

title: Indoor Fan Motor No Feedback
shortDescription: The indoor fan motor is not sending speed feedback signals to the control board.
fullDescription: Error H6 means the indoor fan motor's hall sensor or feedback circuit is not communicating with the PCB, so the board cannot confirm the fan is running.
symptoms: Indoor fan may or may not be spinning

fixSteps.title: Reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check motor connector
fixSteps.instruction: Open indoor unit and check the fan motor plug on the PCB. It may have multiple pins including feedback wires.

fixSteps.title: Test motor manually
fixSteps.instruction: With a direct voltage test, check if the motor runs. If it runs but no feedback, the hall sensor is bad.

fixSteps.title: Replace fan motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the feedback circuit in the motor is failed, the entire motor assembly needs replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the motor needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC H6 fan motor no feedback error fix

faq.q: What is hall sensor feedback?
faq.a: DC fan motors use hall sensors to report speed to the control board for precise speed control.

faq.q: Can a loose connector cause H6?
faq.a: Yes, simply reseating the motor connector can fix H6 in many cases.

faq.q: Is the motor replacement difficult?
faq.a: Moderate difficulty. The barrel fan must be removed to access the motor.

title: Jumper Cap Error
shortDescription: The outdoor unit jumper cap configuration is incorrect or missing.
fullDescription: Error C5 on Gree units indicates the jumper cap on the outdoor unit PCB is missing or set incorrectly. This cap configures the unit for the correct model and refrigerant type.

causes.cause: Jumper cap set to wrong position

fixSteps.title: Check the jumper cap
fixSteps.instruction: Open the outdoor unit electrical cover and locate the jumper cap on the PCB. Ensure it is present and in the correct position.

fixSteps.title: Refer to wiring diagram
fixSteps.instruction: Check the wiring diagram on the inside of the outdoor unit cover for the correct jumper position.

fixSteps.title: Replace if missing
fixSteps.instruction: If the jumper cap is missing, install a replacement in the correct position.

fixSteps.title: Reset and test
fixSteps.instruction: After correcting the jumper, reset the system and test.

whenToCallPro: If the PCB circuit is faulty.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC C5 jumper cap error fix

faq.q: What is the jumper cap?
faq.a: A small plastic cap on the PCB that configures the unit for its specific model and refrigerant.

faq.q: Can a missing jumper cap prevent operation?
faq.a: Yes, the unit will not start without the correct jumper configuration.

faq.q: Is this a common issue?
faq.a: C5 usually occurs after a service visit where the cap was accidentally removed.

title: Outdoor EEPROM Error
shortDescription: The outdoor unit EEPROM memory has a data error.
fullDescription: Error E9 indicates the outdoor PCB EEPROM memory chip has corrupted data, preventing proper system configuration and operation.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at breaker for 10 minutes to allow full EEPROM reinitialize.

fixSteps.title: Install surge protector
fixSteps.instruction: Use a surge protector to prevent future EEPROM corruption.

fixSteps.title: Inspect outdoor PCB
fixSteps.instruction: Check the outdoor PCB for burned or damaged components.

fixSteps.title: Replace outdoor PCB
fixSteps.instruction: If EEPROM is corrupted beyond recovery, replace the outdoor PCB.

whenToCallPro: If the PCB needs replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Gree AC E9 EEPROM outdoor PCB error fix

faq.q: Can power surges cause E9?
faq.a: Yes, power surges are the most common cause of EEPROM corruption.

faq.q: Is E9 fixable?
faq.a: Sometimes a power reset can reinitialize the EEPROM. If not, PCB replacement is needed.

faq.q: Is the outdoor PCB expensive?
faq.a: Gree outdoor PCBs typically cost $80-$200.
